_comment: Out-of-band, on-demand mutation testing (advisory) — `npm run test:mutation` (issue #164). Measures TEST EFFICACY, the axis the 100% coverage gate can't see: it injects faults into the in-scope modules and checks the suite KILLS them (a surviving mutant is a covered-but-weakly-asserted line). NOT a CI gate and NOT part of `npm test` — the nightly/workflow_dispatch advisory job is a separate, currently-blocked follow-up (#166). SCOPE: pure-logic modules only (`mutate` below). Stryker's vitest-runner re-runs vitest under instrumentation many times; the @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers pool (live miniflare/D1 per file) is not a fit for that loop, so mutation is scoped to modules whose specs run in a plain node vitest config (vitest.stryker.config.ts — no Cloudflare plugin). EXCLUDED for now (their tests genuinely need the workers pool): src/lib/auth.ts (real workerd WebCrypto/PBKDF2), src/lib/users.ts + src/ingest/** run/db/validate (D1), and all .astro templates (logic-only per #164). Re-scope here as more pure modules become plain-node testable.
tsconfigFile_comment: Pointed at a name NOT in the project so Stryker skips its tsconfig-rewrite preprocessor. That rewrite does `import('typescript')`, but this repo has no direct `typescript` devDep (it uses Astro's bundled TS), so the default (tsconfig.json) crashes with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND. The rewrite isn't needed: the vitest-runner type-strips via esbuild, and the in-scope modules have no project references / path-relative `extends` to fix up.
ignorePatterns_comment: Keep the Stryker sandbox copy lean and skip agent/scratch dirs. `.codex/skills` is a symlink to a directory, which Stryker's sandbox copy (copyfile) can't handle (EISDIR); excluding the dot-dirs and build/test artifacts also speeds up the copy. node_modules is symlinked into the sandbox automatically, so the in-scope source/tests/configs are all Stryker needs.
